exemplar.title: To what extent was the British-led reconfiguration of borders after World War I the principal catalyst for the emergence of ethnic tensions and conflicts among Iraqâs diverse population?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent was the British-led reconfiguration of borders after World War I the principal catalyst for the emergence of ethnic tensions and conflicts among Iraqâs diverse population?

strengths: Research question is clear, focused and drives the entire investigation.
Introduction enumerates and plans an appropriate range of primary and secondary sources.
Purpose and focus are communicated accurately and maintained throughout the essay.
weaknesses: Methodology evaluation is limited to only two sources with inconsistent footnote practice.
Minor stylistic slips and occasional drift into later twentieth-century context.
Linkages between some contextual sections (e.g., Kurdish autonomy) and the central question could be more explicit.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place topic communication and RQ clarity in the top band (5â6), with methodology largely complete but showing only some evidence of informed selection. The consistent, focused question and effective range of sources justify a high mark in Focus and Method, but minor stylistic and methodological evaluation gaps prevent the full 6 points.

strengths: Source material is mostly relevant and integrated coherently to address the research question.
Demonstrates a clear understanding of mandate politics and key actors such as Gertrude Bell.
Subject-specific terminology (e.g., âmandateâ, âdivide-and-ruleâ, âsectarianismâ) is used correctly.
weaknesses: Some broad historical assertions lack precise citation and rely on tertiary websites.
Repeated spelling and grammatical errors (e.g., âIrags,â misuse of capitals) disrupt consistency.
Limited use of advanced conceptual frameworks or deeper source application in places.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall performance aligns with the upper half of the 3â4 band: knowledge is clear and coherent, sources are generally used effectively, but occasional inaccuracies, inconsistent referencing, and partial application of advanced concepts constrain the score to 4 out of 6.

strengths: Research consistently appropriate and clearly relevant to the research question.
Argument is well-structured, focused, and leads to reasoned conclusions that revisit the central question.
Effective connections drawn between mandate policies and modern instability demonstrate analytical depth.
weaknesses: Evaluation of source bias remains descriptive rather than critically comparative.
Analysis often asserts conclusions without systematic evidence contrast (e.g., economic vs. political drivers).
Further development of long-term implications and deeper critique of alternative interpretations is needed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation shows a sustained, coherent argument with mostly supported conclusions and relevant research, fitting the top of the 4â6 band. While critical evaluation could be deeper, the workâs consistent relevance, analytical connections, and focused discussion merit the full 6 points in Critical Thinking.

strengths: Report structure is clearly appropriate with all required elements (title page, contents, sections).
Layout considerations (word count, page numbering, tables) are present and correctly applied.
Table of contents and section headings aid navigation and coherence.
weaknesses: Footnote formatting is inconsistent and sometimes displayed as raw HTML.
Bibliography entries vary in style and order; URLs frequently break across lines.
Minor layout quirks (abrupt page breaks, stray OCR artifacts) detract from polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets the full expectations of the 3â4 band: structure and formatting are generally correct and all required elements are included. Despite multiple formatting inconsistencies, these are minor and do not obscure comprehension, justifying the top mark of 4 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Word count, title, and page numbers are supplied and tables are generally tidy.  Footnote formatting is inconsistent and some URLs break awkwardly, but overall layout considerations are âpresent and correctly appliedâ more often than not, meriting 3.

A clear argument is maintainedâBritain was a major but not sole catalystâand the conclusion revisits this stance.  Evaluation of sources is brief and largely limited to potential editing bias; there is little critical comparison of perspectives.  Hence the work shows âreasoned argument â¦ partial critical evaluation,â placing it securely in 4-6 but not beyond.

The student presents a precise, manageable research question in the first lines and reiterates it throughout, making the purpose and focus unmistakably clear.  The wording is appropriate for historical investigation and centres on a clearly bounded place, period and issue.  Minor stylistic slips do not hinder comprehension, so the descriptor for 5-6 is best matched, though the communication is not so polished as to justify full marks.

The report follows a logical sequence with title page, contents list and sections that mirror the research focus, satisfying the IA structural expectations.  Minor flawsâduplicated headings, abrupt page breaksâprevent the higher mark, so 3 within the top band is appropriate.

More than a dozen primary and secondary sources are cited and a short âEvaluation of Sourcesâ section is included, showing conscious selection.  Nevertheless, footnote practice is inconsistent, only two sources are formally evaluated, and there is little explanation of criteria for choosing the wider source base.  This fulfils âmostly complete â¦ some evidence of informed selection,â fitting the 3-4 band; the breadth justifies the upper value.

The investigation draws chiefly on primary correspondence, official memoranda and League minutesâappropriate and largely relevant.  There is some weaker, journalistic material but the bulk remains suitable, so most (though not all) research meets the 4-6 standard; a mid-band 5 reflects this balance.

The student links British policies to later ethnic grievances and weighs counter-claims that pre-existing tensions also mattered.  Nevertheless, analysis often rests on assertion (âshowed the British reliance on military powerâ) without systematically contrasting evidence, so conclusions are only âmostly supported.â  This aligns with 4-6, nearer the lower end.

Quotations from Bell, Wilson, RAF reports and League records are integrated to support points, and most are relevant to the question.  However, several citations are repeated newspaper digests or tertiary websites, and some claims lack precise referencing.  Relevance is therefore âmostlyâ rather than âclearlyâ sustained, matching 3-4 with a score of 4.

The narrative demonstrates solid grasp of mandate politics, ethnic composition and the 1920 revolt, moving beyond description by locating events within imperial strategy.  Yet treatment of internal Iraqi agency is patchy and some generalisations (âthe British had decreasing careâ) are unsubstantiated, so the understanding is coherent but not consistently sophisticatedâappropriate for the upper half of band 3-4.

The research question is stated verbatim, is sharply focused and drives the structure of the essay.  Each section is overtly linked back to whether British border-drawing was the âprincipal catalyst,â satisfying the requirement for a clear and appropriately focused question.  A small amount of drift into later twentieth-century developments prevents a top-band 6.

Key terms such as âmandate,â âdivide-and-rule,â and âsectarianismâ are deployed correctly, yet there are repeated spelling/grammatical errors (e.g., âIrags,â âthe quardianâ) and occasional misuse of capitals (Shia/Shi'a).  Consistency is therefore partial, meriting the lower end of the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: Footer page numbering is consistent, supporting reader orientation throughout the document.
The opening paragraph provides context but makes broad claims without citations; add references for historical assertions.
The introduction enumerates primary sources to be used, demonstrating methodology awareness and planning.
Background paragraph ends abruptly mid-sentence; ensure complete sentences to maintain coherence.
Superscript formatting appears as raw HTML entities in footnotes; correct this to improve readability.
The discussion of Gertrude Bellâs correspondence is well integrated and supports the argument coherently.
Website citations vary in format and lack consistency; adopt a single citation style for all sources.
The quote from RAF reports is truncated and lacks context; include the full passage and comment on its significance.
Bellâs characterization of Sunni elites is well evidenced, but evaluate alternative interpretations for balance.
The conclusion in this section effectively synthesizes evidence, linking back to the research question.
Ensure superscript numbers follow punctuation rather than preceding it to align with citation standards.
The title is clearly stated at the outset, framing the investigation effectively.
The research question is presented prominently and drives the inquiry focus throughout the essay.
The student provides a clear word count early in the document, satisfying examination requirements.
The table of contents organizes sections clearly and aids navigation, reflecting thoughtful presentation.
Footnote numbering repeats <sup>4</sup> twice; ensure each citation has a unique identifier for clarity.
Analysis of British oil motivations is descriptive; develop deeper critique of economic versus political drivers.
The phrase "To add a thought" is informal; revise for academic tone and clarity.
Analysis links military power to societal divisions but stops short of evaluating long-term implications; deepen this evaluation.
The overview of ethnic groups is succinct but lacks citation; reference a scholarly source to underpin these descriptions.
The Kurdish autonomy discussion offers good context but needs explicit linkage to the central research question.
In âConflicts and Tensionsâ section, a clear thesis statement would orient the reader to the analysis that follows.
Footnote callouts lack superscript styling and are inconsistently placed; adjust formatting to match academic conventions.
The stray mathematical marker <math>14</math> appears in the conclusion footer; remove OCR artifacts to polish presentation.
Bibliography entries are inconsistently formatted; apply uniform style (e.g., italics for titles, consistent punctuation).
The bibliography is comprehensive in scope but could be better organized alphabetically by author surname.
Several web URLs run across lines, disrupting readability; adjust line breaks or use shortened DOI links.
Ensure digital archive URLs are current and accessible; verify each link prior to submission.
The legacy discussion connects historical mandate policies to modern instability, showcasing analytical depth.
The reflection on future regime patterns hints at engagement but lacks explicit personal insight or planning commentary.
Only two sources are formally evaluated; expand the evaluation to include criteria for selection and reliability.
The source evaluation is descriptive; include analytical commentary on each sourceâs limitations and biases.
